rationale: The cited paper is not a generic background fit: it directly studies channel governance mechanisms (selection/socialization/incentives/monitoring, including dual distribution) and their interaction effects on outcomes, which aligns with the citing paper’s core focus on contingent governance-by-channel-usage performance effects. The DOI match (10.1509/jmr.14.0182) confirms this specific governance study is the referenced work.
